summary: Soundboard recording of decent clarity, but the show is marred by Jerry’s abrupt Terrapin walk‑off – China Cat▶Rider shines while the rest feels average to poor.
review: The Walker/Miller SBD capture is generally clear and well‑balanced, though a few listeners note slight bias issues. Musically the concert is most remembered for an excellent China Cat▶Rider and a beautiful Desolation Row solo, but Jerry’s sudden exit during Terrapin Station leaves the second set uneven and many fans consider the overall performance only average.

ai_rating.rationale: Recording quality is consistently praised (≈4 ★) while show quality ranges from neutral to negative (≈2.8 ★). Weighted combination yields ~3.2 ★.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Walker's Miller SBD is praised as one of the cleanest uploads; listeners note crispness but a few mention bias/volume quirks in some copies.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set is average with a few highlights; the second set peaks with China Cat▶Rider but collapses after Jerry abruptly walks off during Terrapin, leaving the band scrambling and the momentum lost.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Abruptly walked off mid‑Terrapin (possible health issue); his solo on Desolation Row is praised; playing turns to rapid scales before exit.
band_member_comments.Bob: Brent Mydland tries to keep the show moving after the walk‑off, adding synth lines in Peggy‑O and later I Don't Need Love.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent’s synth work noted on Peggy‑O and Supplication; generally solid but unable to rescue the set after Jerry leaves.
band_member_comments.Drums: Bill/Mickey (drummers) did not successfully take over Terrapin, contributing to the feeling of a stalled second half.
